name: container-alpine-clean
description: Maintained, currently-patched Alpine image with no actionable (high/critical) CVEs. FP-control / specificity check — tsengine must not raise an actionable finding on a clean target. POINT-IN-TIME baseline: a 'clean' image rots as new base-image CVEs are disclosed (e.g. 3.18 accrued musl CVE-2026-40200, an unfixed real CVE — a TRUE positive, not an engine FP), so this target is refreshed to a currently-patched tag rather than pinned forever. Verified clean (0 high+) 2026-06-28.

competitors.leaderboard: Trivy / Snyk Container / Anchore — self-published only
competitors.note: Negative (specificity) test case: a clean image should produce zero high/critical CVE findings (harmless info-level base-image notes are tolerated — the severity-gated FP floor, not a brittle total-count cap). Pairs with container-nginx-vuln (sensitivity) for a recall + FP-rate read on container scanning. FP bar tracks the XBOW 'no false positives' standard. Refresh the target tag when a new base CVE lands against it (the same world-moves discipline as the threat-intel corpus, CLAUDE.md §7) — do NOT suppress the engine, which is correctly reporting a true positive.
